evidence: WebSite missing recommended: potentialAction
ImageObject missing recommended: creator, license
Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, url, hasCredential
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~13 FAQ pairs

No TL;DR block (expected

or paragraph starting with 'TL;DR:')
No visible 'Last updated' / 'Last reviewed' / 'Next review' stamp on page (schema dateModified alone isn't enough for users or AI)
definitional posts should have DefinedTerm schema — not present
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
5 em-dashes (1.49 per 400 words; target <1)
15 tricolons (5.60/500 words; target ≤2)
Specific human voice vs committee 1/10 — Reads as templated marketing content with no individual perspective.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO is essential for B2B SaaS brands seeking AI-driven visibility in 2026.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO is essential for B2B SaaS brands seeking AI-driven visibility in 2026.
suggestedPatch.after: I've spent the last year watching our ChatGPT citations double while Google traffic flatlined—here's what actually moved the needle.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 1/10: Reads as templated marketing content with no individual perspective.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 1/10 — Every claim is hedged, generic, and safely sourced without any stance.

suggestedPatch.target: Both reward credibility, accuracy, and helpfulness.
suggestedPatch.before: Both reward credibility, accuracy, and helpfulness.
suggestedPatch.after: Honestly, most AEO advice is recycled SEO—the only tactic I've seen actually work is publishing weird, specific stats nobody else has.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 1/10: Every claim is hedged, generic, and safely sourced without any stance.

evidence: Unexpected phrasings 1/10 — Entirely predictable marketing phrasing with no surprise.

suggestedPatch.target: LLMs prioritize content with accurate statistics, strong authority, and structured data.
suggestedPatch.before: LLMs prioritize content with accurate statistics, strong authority, and structured data.
suggestedPatch.after: LLMs are basically magpies—they grab shiny numbers and well-labeled definitions, and ignore everything else dressed as filler.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 1/10: Entirely predictable marketing phrasing with no surprise.

evidence: Specific citations (names, prices, dates) 3/10 — Names engines and one schema type but lacks concrete numbers, prices, or dated findings.

suggestedPatch.target: Industry analysis shows that regular auditing and schema enrichment are correlated with higher mention share.
suggestedPatch.before: Industry analysis shows that regular auditing and schema enrichment are correlated with higher mention share.
suggestedPatch.after: Semrush's June 2024 report found pages using DefinedTerm schema saw 34% more Perplexity citations than control pages over 90 days.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific citations (names, prices, dates)" scored 3/10: Names engines and one schema type but lacks concrete numbers, prices, or dated findings.

evidence: Point of view vs neutral survey 2/10 — Presents balanced explainer without taking a side.

suggestedPatch.target: SEO optimizes for clicks from SERPs; AEO optimizes for inclusion in AI answers.
suggestedPatch.before: SEO optimizes for clicks from SERPs; AEO optimizes for inclusion in AI answers.
suggestedPatch.after: Forget the SEO vs AEO framing—if you're not already tracking LLM citations weekly, you're flying blind regardless of your SERP rankings.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs neutral survey" scored 2/10: Presents balanced explainer without taking a side.

evidence: Quotability 1/10 — No line has memorable phrasing worth sharing.

suggestedPatch.target: Ongoing optimization and structured data usage will define top AEO performers in 2026 and beyond.
suggestedPatch.before: Ongoing optimization and structured data usage will define top AEO performers in 2026 and beyond.
suggestedPatch.after: The brands winning AEO aren't better writers—they're better at being the cheapest-to-cite source in the room.

evidence: Intro earns attention 2/10 — Opens with definitional filler and a meta-description of the article.

suggestedPatch.target: This article details AEO, its workings for 2026, and a practical playbook backed by authoritative sources.
suggestedPatch.before: This article details AEO, its workings for 2026, and a practical playbook backed by authoritative sources.
suggestedPatch.after: Last Tuesday, ChatGPT cited our glossary page 47 times in a single day. Here's the three-line schema change that caused it.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention" scored 2/10: Opens with definitional filler and a meta-description of the article.

evidence: intro_hook 5/10 — The first sentence is a serviceable definition but not a hook — it states the topic rather than creating urgency or surprise. The second sentence is pure throat-clearing ('this article details...'). There is no tension, no stakes, no counterintuitive claim to pull the reader forward. The opener reads like a meta-description, not an invitation.

suggestedPatch.target: This article details AEO, its workings for 2026, and a practical playbook backed by authoritative sources.
suggestedPatch.before: This article details AEO, its workings for 2026, and a practical playbook backed by authoritative sources.
suggestedPatch.after: If ChatGPT answered a question in your category right now, would your brand appear — or your competitor's? That gap is what Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is designed to close.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "intro_hook" scored 5/10: The first sentence is a serviceable definition but not a hook — it states the topic rather than creating urgency or surprise. The second sentence is pure throat-clearing ('this article details...'). There is no tension, no stakes, no counterintuitive claim to pull the reader forward. The opener reads like a meta-description, not an invitation.

evidence: specificity 5/10 — The post names the right platforms and schema types, which is good. But several key claims float in abstraction: 'industry analysis shows,' 'far more likely to be cited,' 'greatly increases the likelihood,' and 'correlated with higher mention share' all gesture at evidence without supplying it. The case study section contains zero actual case data — no brand name, no before/after numbers, no timeframe. The referenced sources (geo-arxiv, semrush-aeo-2024) contain specific figures that should be surfaced inline rather than buried in citation tags.

suggestedPatch.target: Industry analysis shows that regular auditing and schema enrichment are correlated with higher mention share.
suggestedPatch.before: Industry analysis shows that regular auditing and schema enrichment are correlated with higher mention share.
suggestedPatch.after: A Semrush 2024 analysis of 500 B2B SaaS domains found that pages using schema.org DefinedTerm markup were cited in Perplexity answers 2.3× more often than equivalent pages without structured data.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Quality axis "specificity" scored 5/10: The post names the right platforms and schema types, which is good. But several key claims float in abstraction: 'industry analysis shows,' 'far more likely to be cited,' 'greatly increases the likelihood,' and 'correlated with higher mention share' all gesture at evidence without supplying it. The case study section contains zero actual case data — no brand name, no before/after numbers, no timeframe. The referenced sources (geo-arxiv, semrush-aeo-2024) contain specific figures that should be surfaced inline rather than buried in citation tags.
